transcript.clean_text: after two spectacular days to kick off parin the Riders prepared for another windy outing between sea and munan iser on stage three 200k of a flat terrain but the pace dropping off significantly after yesterday's frantic second stage where there was an average speed of over 51 km an hour Andre griel and Roman B both describing it as one of their toughest ever days in the saddle the speed dropping to 34.5 K an hour in the first two hours today but another major abandon in the shape of Fabio aru the Italian over 17 minutes down overall feeling weak and suffering with nausea according to UAE team Emirates two men getting away at the 40K Mark rinas nacas and allesandro fideli both of Delco Mar the experienced Lithuanian and his young Italian teammate over 5 and a half minutes clear at one points the pelan battling a headwind as they gradually close the gap infinitely preferable to the Cross wins that caused it so much chaos on stages 1 and two the Sprint team's playing it safe and catching up to the two escapes with 39K to go but there was a bit of drama a few minutes later a crash in the bunch going over a narrow bridge and poor old Mark cair was one of those stuck in the bottleneck the Spaniard won't be defending his crown this year team Sky once again hitting the front in the closing stages Road Captain Luke Rog guiding Mel kovsky and Egan Bernal through the intermediate Sprint for more bonus seconds and so heading towards the first and likely the last proper Bunch prints of this year's race Dyan grunen was looking to become just the second man to win three consecutive stages at parin but the Dutchman was denied Sam Bennett simply had too much in the tank the Irishman holding off Caleb huan to claim the stage three win the number three is a significant one for Bennett he also won stage three of parin two years ago and this is his third win of the season after stage victories on the UAE tour and the tour of San Juan the 28-year-old also won three stages at last year's jro ditalia and he could perhaps be another weapon for Border hand grower alongside Peter San at this year's T of France yeah today the legs were a lot better okay it was an easier day but uh starting to get more used to the cold it's not that cold but it's it's colder and um I knew today was my last real sprint opportunity so uh did everything I could and uh the boys did a fantastic job so Bennett the master of mulah Caleb euan and Fabio yakobson in second and third just as they were on stage one Dan mcleay fourth for Education First ahead of Frenchman Brian Cocker grun ofen down in ninth place no real changes in the GC barring a few bonus seconds here and there grun ofen stays in yellow ahead of K kovsky and LS Leon Sanchez but we can expect a big shake up in the standings tomorrow the favorites will come to the four on stage four the longest of the race 212 bumpy kilm between vishy and Pusa with a real sting in the tail four classified climbs in the final 60k it promises to be a cracker so do join us again on Wednesday and thanks for watching
